episode_summary: The team dive into audience-sent curiosities about sumo tournaments throwing thousands of kilos of salt, a Swedish parking lot said to contain 10,000 buried accordions, CPR training dummies with breasts to counter bystander hesitancy toward aiding women, and experimental eye drops from pig semen delivering cancer treatment to mice.

facts.fact: Matthew says that each year professional sumo tournaments in Japan consume about 4,000 kilograms of salt.
There are 10,000 accordions under a parking lot in a Swedish town called Alvdelen.
And her fact is that CPR training dummies with boobs on them are now being used at surf lifesaving clubs in New South Wales because women are less likely to receive CPR.
It's by Auri Fulger and Auri says that eye drops made from pig semen deliver cancer treatment to mice.
